questions.q: In which year did Gandhi lead the Champaran Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: The Champaran Satyagraha took place in 1917, Gandhi's first major campaign in India.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Champaran located?
questions.explanation: Champaran is a district in the northern part of Bihar.

questions.q: The Champaran Satyagraha was undertaken to help cultivators of which crop?
questions.explanation: The campaign supported indigo cultivators oppressed by European planters.

questions.q: Champaran is significant because it was Gandhi's first major satyagraha on which soil?
questions.explanation: Champaran established Gandhi's method of nonviolent resistance on Indian soil for the first time on a major scale.

questions.q: The oppressive system forcing peasants to grow indigo at Champaran was called what?
questions.explanation: Under the tinkathia system, peasants were compelled to cultivate indigo on a fixed portion of their land.

questions.q: Who led the Champaran Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi led the Champaran campaign in 1917.

questions.q: Who chiefly oppressed the indigo peasants of Champaran?
questions.explanation: European indigo planters exploited the peasants through forced cultivation and unfair levies.

questions.q: The success of the Champaran campaign helped establish Gandhi's method known as what?
questions.explanation: Champaran demonstrated the power of satyagraha, Gandhi's technique of nonviolent resistance.

questions.q: Which peasant of Champaran is credited with persuading Gandhi to visit and take up the cause?
questions.explanation: The cultivator Raj Kumar Shukla repeatedly urged Gandhi to come to Champaran and investigate the planters' oppression.

questions.q: What was the broad aim of the Champaran Satyagraha?
options: To relieve the peasants from forced indigo cultivation and unjust exactions
questions.explanation: The campaign sought to free indigo cultivators from forced planting and to redress their grievances against the planters.

questions.q: Under the tinkathia system, peasants had to grow indigo on what fraction of their holding?
questions.explanation: The term 'tinkathia' referred to growing indigo on three kathas out of every twenty, roughly three-twentieths of the land.

questions.q: When Gandhi reached Champaran, the local authorities ordered him to do what?
questions.explanation: Officials served Gandhi an order to leave Champaran, which he refused to obey, accepting the legal consequences.

questions.q: Which future first President of India assisted Gandhi during the Champaran campaign?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad, who later became India's first President, was among the lawyers who joined Gandhi at Champaran.

questions.q: When ordered to leave, Gandhi chose to do what rather than comply?
options: Disobey the order and face trial
questions.explanation: Gandhi disobeyed the order to leave and presented himself for trial, marking an act of civil disobedience.

questions.q: The official body set up to look into the peasants' grievances was called what?
questions.explanation: The government appointed the Champaran Agrarian Inquiry Committee, on which Gandhi served as a member.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the official inquiry into the Champaran grievances?
options: Legislation curbing the planters' exactions was enacted
questions.explanation: The inquiry led to the Champaran Agrarian Act, which abolished the tinkathia system and provided relief to the peasants.

questions.q: Which lawyer, later a Congress leader and Gandhian, was an important local supporter at Champaran?
questions.explanation: Brij Kishore Prasad was a prominent local lawyer who supported the Champaran campaign alongside Rajendra Prasad.

questions.q: Gandhi's approach at Champaran included carefully doing what before pressing demands?
options: Recording detailed testimony from thousands of peasants
Calling a general strike across India
questions.explanation: Gandhi methodically collected statements from thousands of peasants to document the planters' abuses.

questions.q: The Champaran campaign reflected Gandhi's broader belief that political work should be combined with what?
questions.explanation: Gandhi paired the agitation with constructive efforts such as schools, sanitation and village improvement in Champaran.

questions.q: The Champaran indigo trade had been undermined economically before 1917 partly because of what development?
questions.explanation: The rise of cheap synthetic dyes had reduced demand for natural indigo, intensifying pressure on the cultivators.

questions.q: Champaran (1917) is often paired with which Gandhian peasant campaign of 1918 as an early Indian satyagraha?
questions.explanation: The Kheda Satyagraha of 1918 in Gujarat followed Champaran as another early Gandhian peasant campaign.

questions.q: The Champaran experience helped Gandhi recruit and train a cadre of future leaders from which region especially?
questions.explanation: Champaran brought forward a generation of Bihar Congress workers, including Rajendra Prasad and others.

questions.q: In which month of 1917 did Gandhi first arrive in Champaran?
questions.explanation: Gandhi reached Champaran in April 1917 to begin his investigation of the indigo peasants' plight.

questions.q: Gandhi was summoned before a magistrate at which town in Champaran when he defied the order to leave?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was tried before the magistrate at Motihari, the district headquarters, after refusing to leave Champaran.

questions.q: Apart from forced cultivation, peasants protested an illegal levy demanded by planters known by what name?
questions.explanation: Planters imposed exactions such as 'sharahbeshi' (enhanced rent) and 'tawan' (compensation), which the campaign opposed.

questions.q: The Champaran Agrarian Committee's recommendations led to a law passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Champaran Agrarian Act, abolishing the tinkathia system, was enacted in 1918 following the committee's report.

questions.q: The educationist and later Congress general secretary J. B. Kripalani assisted Gandhi at Champaran while teaching at which place?
questions.explanation: J. B. Kripalani, then a teacher at Muzaffarpur, was among the volunteers who helped Gandhi during the Champaran campaign.

questions.q: Roughly what proportion of the planters' illegally enhanced exactions did the settlement require to be refunded to peasants?
questions.explanation: Under the settlement the planters agreed to refund about a quarter of the illegal exactions, a symbolic but significant concession.

questions.q: Which Bihari Muslim leader and lawyer was also an early supporter who worked with Gandhi at Champaran?
questions.explanation: Mazharul Haque, a prominent Bihar barrister, was among the local leaders who supported Gandhi at Champaran.

questions.q: The case against Gandhi for disobeying the order to leave Champaran ended in what manner?
options: Gandhi was jailed for two years
Gandhi was acquitted after a long trial
The case was withdrawn by the government
questions.explanation: Recognizing the strength of public feeling, the government withdrew the case against Gandhi, allowing him to continue the inquiry.
